rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL JOSEPH COSTELLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (2) What does "Debit Misc." check order mean? (3) Please explain the disbursements for "Firstbrook Cassie Ande, wire transfer and wife transfer fees." (4) For what year or taxes was the FTB paid on 8/10/22? (5) What happened to the CIBC GIC accounts? (6) What happened with the US Oil & Gas PLC shares (shown as a loss on sale)? (7) Attorney's e-filing costs should be itemized, showing what was e-filed for each fee. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/1/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Although paragraph 13 states that the accounting period begins 12/9/22, it appears to correctly begin on the date of death. Supporting accounting schedules must include the date, source or payee, the description of the receipt or payment, and amount: in other words, there should be four distinct columns.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT A. STEPICEVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. 1) This second amended petition was not e-filed until 5/22/24. Notice was reportedly mailed on 5/17/24. Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed, when it is actually viewable by an interested party. [LRSF 14.14 A.] 2) It is unclear why any supplemental or escape property taxes have not yet been determined and why $4,000 needs to be set aside. The accounting shows $3,030.80 already paid to the SF Tax Collector on 9/15/23 for property tax. 3) The creditor's claim filed by Felicia Zurich was for $2,164.26. The disbursements schedule shows that she was paid $2,346.49, or $182.23 more than she was entitled to. 4) The statutory fee calculation includes just the $65,000 loss on the sale of the real property, but the accounting shows losses on sale totaling $135,244.10. The statutory compensation should be no more than $25,235.06. 5) A personal representative is responsible for preparing his own accounting. If a third party was paid to prepare the accounting, that amount must be deducted from the administrator's statutory compensation. 6) Administrator now requests to set aside another $1,500 for renewal of his bond. Had the account and report been correct in February, and had Administrator not invested estate assets in speculative brokered CDs but invested cash in FDIC insured bank accounts, the estate could have closed must earlier. 7) Paragraph 4 of the petition claims that the assets on hand total $1,193,231.93, all cash, but that was the beginning balance of assets on hand, according to the summary of account, which included real property and tangible personal property. 8) Paragraph 19 of the petition shows that the I&As filed total $1,251,454.42, but the accounting begins with $1,193,231.93. It appears that the administrator forgot to include the MetLife insurance proceeds he reported on a Supplemental I&A filed on 9/27/23 and accounted for it as a receipt instead. 9) If the cost to publish notice was $215 on 9/28/22, it is unclear why, if $35 was paid from the $700 retainer Felicia Zurich paid to the attorney, the attorney then paid an additional $193.23 to the Daily Journal for the second publication on 10/19/22. 10) For some reason, some of the heirs' shares on Exhibit G to the second amended petition show that they are entitled to 1/4 of the estate. 11) The verification of this pleading just refers to "the foregoing Petition," which is not exactly an accurate verification of the pleading being verified.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS J OERTEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/9/24. (No earlier availability.) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Please provide a courtesy copy of the petitioner's signed and verified supplement to his petition, addressing this issue, to the assigned examiner by 6/28/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The petition requests that bond be set at $600,000. Item 5a of the proposed order was checked, indicating "Bond is not required," but $600,000 bond will be required unless the petition is amended because all heirs have waived bond. If the petition is amended, notice of the amendment must be mailed and published.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW SHUEN CHARK LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: 1. Need information on the parent (who is a sibling of the decedent) of each nephew - whether they are children of Gwendolyn Lam or Arthur Lam. 2. Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to be given to the persons entitled. LRSF 14.1.C.6) PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
